text: Traffic flow prediction is an essential part of the core technology of Intelligent Transportation System. Although there are a large number of studies that have explored traffic flow prediction in the past decade, one challenging issue is to model the underlying complex spatial-temporal patterns in traffic data. Recently, the application of spatial-temporal Graph Neural Network (GNN) in traffic flow prediction has become very popular, such as STSGNN, S-GCN, Graphwavenet and ST-GCN. While those methods have demonstrated their superior performance in terms of accuracy and speed, there are still many limitations for being applied in the real world, which are listed as follows: 1) Most existing methods rely heavily on the assumption that the spatial relationships of urban traffic network are static, which cannot keep up with changing traffic patterns; 2) Most graph-based models tend to focus only on the local connections within an urban traffic network, while overlooking the impact of distant locations; 3) The evolution of the local traffic conditions in an area is influenced not only by its neighboring areas, but also by those located in distant parts of the network, and therefore the traffic evolution between different graph locations is subject to certain lag or delay time. Therefore, it is of great necessity to design an efficient graph-based model which overcomes the aforementioned three shortcomings, and hence we propose a novel Propagation Delay-aware dynamic long-range transFormer, namely PDFormer, to make up for the existing shortcomings. Experiments based on six distinct real-world public traffic datasets are conducted, and the results show that the proposed PDFormer model significantly advances the state-of-the-art performance, meanwhile, the running speed is close to the leading models in spite of the much larger model size. Additionally, the attention map of our proposed model has been shown as the visualization proof to confirm the interpretability of the designed model.
